commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair lane is needed because the earlier matched-key blocker is fixed; remaining action is normal maintainer merge and proof judgment.; Cleared: No dependency, workflow, lockfile, permission, or new secret-storage surface is introduced; the diff uses existing SecretRef/runtime-snapshot APIs without logging secret values. commands.review_summary:
The PR resolves unresolved Feishu appId/appSecret SecretRefs before outbound Feishu payload, text, media, and message-tool sends build SDK clients, with regression coverage for exec/Keychain refs and mixed-case account keys.

PR surface: Source +118, Tests +158. Total +276 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main throws when an exec/file SecretRef reaches strict Feishu runtime account resolution, and outbound media catches media failures and falls back to text/URL. I did not run a live Feishu tenant or Keychain scenario.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed because the earlier matched-key blocker is fixed; remaining action is normal maintainer merge and proof judgment.

target.title: fix(feishu): resolve exec/keychain appSecret SecretRefs on the outbound path
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #89338 reports that the Feishu **MEDIA** directive (and image/file uploads in general) silently fails when `appSecret` is configured as a `SecretRef` backed by an `exec`/Keychain provider. Normal chat (WebSocket connection) and text replies keep working; only the media upload is dropped, so the recipient receives no image. The regression appeared after migrating credentials from plaintext to a Keychain-backed `SecretRef`.
- **Root Cause**: Feishu resolves app credentials per outbound API call through `resolveFeishuRuntimeAccount` → `resolveFeishuSecretLike`, a **synchronous** resolver that only understands plaintext strings and `env`-source SecretRefs. For an `exec`/`file`/Keychain `SecretRef` it throws `FeishuSecretRefUnavailableError` (its own message says the value must be *resolved against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it*). The WebSocket connection and text replies receive a config in which `appSecret` was already resolved to a string, so they work; the media-upload delivery path can reach `feishuOutbound` with a config in which `appSecret` is still an unresolved `SecretRef`. The sync resolver then throws, the throw is caught in `feishuOutbound.sendMedia` (and the local-image path in `sendText`), and the send degrades to a plain URL/text fallback — the "silent" failure the reporter saw. Plaintext worked because a plaintext value is returned immediately, bypassing SecretRef resolution entirely.
- **Fix**: Resolve still-unresolved app-credential SecretRefs at the Feishu outbound seam before the SDK client is built. A new helper `resolveFeishuOutboundCredentialsConfig` inspects the outbound account's `appId`/`appSecret`; if either is still a `SecretRef`, it first reuses the value the gateway already resolved into the active runtime snapshot (`getRuntimeConfigSnapshot`) — so an `exec`/Keychain secret resolved at connection time is not re-run per send — and only falls back to the async, exec/Keychain-capable SDK resolver (`resolveConfiguredSecretInputString`) for a ref the snapshot does not cover (e.g. a standalone CLI process). The resolved string is inlined into a shallow copy of the config (at the account level when an accounts map exists, top-level otherwise, so an implicit single-account config does not gain a spurious accounts map). When inlining at the account level it patches the **existing matched account-map key** (which may be noncanonical / mixed-case) rather than the normalized id, so the account's other per-account settings are not shadowed by a new entry. Plaintext/already-resolved configs are returned unchanged with no SDK secret resolver call and no clone. The three `feishuOutbound` entry points (`sendPayload`, `sendText`, `sendMedia`) and the agent message-tool `send`/`thread-reply` action handler (`channel.ts`, covering its card / media / text branches) call it first, so the existing synchronous downstream resolution now sees a resolved string and the send proceeds.
- **What changed**:
  - `extensions/feishu/src/accounts.ts` — add `resolveFeishuOutboundCredentialsConfig` (and a small `resolveFeishuCredentialRefValue` helper) that resolves unresolved `appId`/`appSecret` SecretRefs — preferring the gateway runtime snapshot (`openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-config-snapshot`), then the async `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input-runtime` resolver — and inlines them into the returned config at the existing matched account-map key. Also extract `findFeishuAccountMapKey` (shared with `resolveRawFeishuAccountConfig`) for the case-insensitive key match.
  - `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts` — resolve credentials at the start of the `feishuOutbound` `sendPayload`, `sendText`, and `sendMedia` handlers before any SDK client is built.
  - `extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts` — resolve credentials once at the start of the message-tool `send`/`thread-reply` action handler and use the resolved config for the card, media, and text branches (the card/text branches do not go through the outbound bridge).
  - `extensions/feishu/src/accounts.test.ts` / `channel.test.ts` — regression cases on the real resolver: the strict runtime account throws on an exec/Keychain `appSecret` SecretRef and succeeds after the helper inlines it; an inherited top-level SecretRef is resolved without inventing an accounts map; the matched noncanonical/mixed-case account key and its per-account settings are preserved; plaintext credentials return the same config object unchanged; an active runtime snapshot value is reused without the async resolver; and a message-tool text `send` with an exec SecretRef reaches `sendMessageFeishu` with the resolved credential.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - No config surface (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`); the on-disk `SecretRef` shape is read, never rewritten.
  - No plugin SDK surface (no SDK exports, manifest, `extensions/*` api/runtime-api, registry/loader); the helper only consumes existing public SDK subpaths (`openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-config-snapshot`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input-runtime`), both already used by other bundled plugins.
  - No dependency/lockfile changes. Gateway protocol unchanged.
  - The synchronous resolver, its error type, the connection/event-secret resolution, and the existing transient-error fallback are untouched; only the outbound delivery path gains an up-front resolution step.

### Reproduction

1. Configure a Feishu account with `appSecret` as an `exec`/Keychain `SecretRef`, e.g. `channels.feishu.accounts.<id>.appSecret = { "source": "exec", "provider": "keychain_feishu_<id>", "id": "value" }`.
2. Restart the gateway; WebSocket chat connects normally.
3. Send a local image via the `MEDIA` directive in a Feishu DM.
4. **Before this PR**: the upload throws `FeishuSecretRefUnavailableError`, is caught, and degrades to a URL/text fallback — no image is delivered.
5. **After this PR**: the SecretRef is resolved against the secret runtime before the client is built, and the image is uploaded normally.

Deterministic source reproduction (added as regression tests): with the reporter's exact `exec` SecretRef shape, `resolveFeishuRuntimeAccount` throws; after `resolveFeishuOutboundCredentialsConfig`, the same call resolves with the inlined secret.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#89338 — Feishu media upload now works with an exec/Keychain SecretRef appSecret)**: outbound delivery resolves unresolved app-credential SecretRefs before building the Feishu client, instead of dropping the upload to a URL/text fallback.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node — Vitest against the real `extensions/feishu/src/accounts.ts` resolution path, with the SDK secret resolver mocked to emulate the gateway secret runtime; no live Feishu tenant)**: verified via `accounts.test.ts`, plus extension production and test type-checks and a format check on the changed files. A live Feishu media send against a real tenant was not run.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch (repo-root)**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/{accounts,channel,outbound}.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json` and `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json`; `oxfmt --check` and `oxlint` on the changed files.

**Evidence after fix (Vitest output for the touched test files)**:

```text
 accounts.test.ts   Tests  28 passed (28)
 channel.test.ts    Tests  62 passed (62)
 outbound.test.ts   Tests  42 passed (42)
```

The new cases prove the regression and its fix on the real resolver: the strict runtime account throws `FeishuSecretRefUnavailableError` on an exec/Keychain `appSecret` SecretRef and then resolves successfully once `resolveFeishuOutboundCredentialsConfig` has inlined it; an inherited top-level SecretRef resolves without inventing an accounts map; the matched noncanonical/mixed-case account key keeps its per-account settings; plaintext credentials return the identical config object (no clone, no SDK secret resolver call); a value already present in the active runtime snapshot is reused without invoking the async resolver; and a message-tool text `send` with an exec SecretRef reaches `sendMessageFeishu` with the resolved credential. (The two test files mock exactly the SDK seams the helper uses — `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input-runtime` and `runtime-config-snapshot` — which is the helper's real public boundary.)

**Observed result after fix (media path no longer drops the upload)**: `resolveFeishuRuntimeAccount` on the resolved config returns `configured: true` with the resolved `appSecret`, so the SDK client builds and the upload proceeds. Extension prod + test type-checks pass; oxlint and oxfmt are clean.

**What was not tested (live Feishu tenant media send)**: a real Feishu DM image send over a live WebSocket session with a Keychain-backed secret was not reproduced; the proof is the deterministic module-level reproduction the issue describes.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: an unresolved-credential outbound send now performs an async secret resolution. **Mitigation**: the resolver is only invoked when `appId`/`appSecret` is still a `SecretRef`; plaintext and already-resolved configs short-circuit with no resolver call and no clone, so the common path is unchanged. When a credential is genuinely unresolvable, the helper leaves the config untouched and the existing transient-error fallback still applies.
- **Risk**: an exec/Keychain secret could be re-run per send. **Mitigation**: the helper first reuses the gateway's already-resolved runtime snapshot (`getRuntimeConfigSnapshot`), so a secret resolved at connection time is not re-executed per send; the async provider resolver is only reached for a ref the snapshot does not cover (e.g. a standalone CLI process without an active gateway snapshot). This reuses an existing prepared runtime object rather than adding new cache state.
- **Risk**: config rewriting could change account selection. **Mitigation**: the resolved value is inlined where the raw value already lives (account-override when an accounts map exists, top-level otherwise) and the original config object is never mutated, covered by the regression tests.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Channels (Feishu)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #89338
target.author: openperf
